commentary:
# Cross-Exchange Liquidity Commentary – 24 July 2026

The ranking clusters into three tiers. The US exchanges dominate: Nasdaq 100 leads decisively with a 0.0024% per $1M ILLIQ score and commanding $290.5M comparable volume, while NYSE ranks second with a 0.0058% per $1M ILLIQ and 0.6280% AR spread. The mid-tier consists of Tokyo Stock Exchange, Euronext, SIX Swiss Exchange, and Hong Kong Stock Exchange, all posting ILLIQ scores between 0.0304% and 0.0446% per $1M traded, though Tokyo's 2.5053% turnover ratio substantially exceeds its peers. The bottom tier—Korea Exchange, Xetra, and London Stock Exchange—suffer from either elevated ILLIQ costs or wider spreads: Korea Exchange's 0.1415% per $1M ILLIQ and 1.5931% AR spread both rank worst, while LSE's 0.0814% per $1M ILLIQ underperforms despite a respectable 0.7973% spread. A notable divergence appears in SIX Swiss Exchange, which boasts the tightest AR spread at 0.5286% yet posts the highest ILLIQ score in the mid-tier at 0.0446% per $1M, suggesting that quoted spreads may overstate actual execution quality for larger trades on that venue.
